# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.145.0/http/server.ts";
import { openKv } from "https://deno.land/x/kv@v0.3.2/mod.ts";

const kvStore = openKv('my_app');
const searchKey = 'search';

interface QueryParams {
  userId?: string;
  filterParam?: string;
}

async function getData(query: QueryParams) {
  const key = [`users`, query.userId, query.filterParam].filter(Boolean);
  if (!key.length) return {};

  const result = await kvStore.get(key.join(','));
  return result || {};
}

serve((req) => {
  if (req.method !== 'GET') return new Response('Method Not Allowed', { status: 405 });

  const url = new URL(req.url);
  const queryParams = new URLSearchParams(url.search);
  const userId = queryParams.get('userId');
  const filterParam = queryParams.get('filterParam');

  if (!userId || !filterParam) return new Response('Missing required parameters', { status: 400 });

  getData({ userId, filterParam })
    .then(data => new Response(JSON.stringify(data), { headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } }))
    .catch(error => new Response(`Error: ${error}`, { status: 500 }));
}, { port: 8000 });
